sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 38-year-old male, presents with a 1-week history of right knee pain after an injury at a trampoline park.
He reports hearing a snap and instant pain when landing, with pain localized to the lateral aspect of the knee.
He denies taking any medication other than Tylenol, which provided minimal relief.
He has not tried icing, heating, or elevating the knee.
Past medical history: The patient has hypertension, for which he is taking 20mg of lisinopril daily, and diabetes, for which he is taking 500mg of metformin.
He denies any other medical conditions.
Review of systems: The patient denies any other symptoms, but mentions that he has been inconsistent with his exercise regimen.
Current medications:
Lisinopril 20mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Tylenol (dose and frequency not specified) for pain
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 180/80, which is higher than desired.
Physical exam: The patient has a slight limp and a gait disturbance, with ecchymosis and edema along the lateral aspect of the knee.
The knee has a decent range of motion, but the patient experiences pain with movement.
Test Results: The patient's right knee x-ray shows no evidence of fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient presents with a right knee ligament strain and hypertension, for which his blood pressure is not well-controlled, and diabetes.
Problem list: Right knee ligament strain; hypertension; diabetes; suspected hypertension.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15mg daily for pain and swelling related to the right knee ligament strain.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent further injury.
I will continue the lisinopril at 20mg daily for hypertension, and order an echo to assess cardiac function.
I also ordered an A1c and lipid panel to assess long-term blood sugar control and lipid levels.
Follow-up: The patient will return for a follow-up appointment to discuss the results of the echo, A1c, and lipid panel, and to reassess the right knee.
